---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-section">
  <div class="hero-content">
    <div class="hero-photo">
      <img src="images/profile.jpg"
           alt="Pandelis Perakakis"
           class="profile-photo">
    </div>
    <div class="hero-text">
      <h1 class="hero-name">Pandelis Perakakis</h1>
      <p class="hero-title">Associate Professor of Psychology</p>
      <p class="hero-affiliation">Complutense University of Madrid<br>
      Dept. of Social, Work and Differential Psychology</p>
      <p class="hero-bio">
        I am an associate professor of psychology at the Complutense University of Madrid,
        teaching Individual Differences. My research encompasses human psychophysiology and
        affective science, with a particular interest in affect shift dynamics and their
        significance for psychological well-being.
      </p>
      <div class="hero-links">
        <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9130-3247" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">
          <i class="bi bi-globe"></i> ORCID
        </a>
        <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=5S-EVSAAAAAJ" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">
          <i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Google Scholar
        </a>
        <a href="https://github.com/perakakis" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">
          <i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub
        </a>
        <a href="https://x.com/ppandelis" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">
          <i class="bi bi-twitter-x"></i> X
        </a>
        <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pandelis-perakakis-1369402a/" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">
          <i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn
        </a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Research Interests

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.research-card}
### Affect Dynamics
Exploring the relation between affect shifts and psychological well-being. Most people don't gradually drift between emotional states — they remain in one state before suddenly tipping into another. Understanding these bistable dynamics has implications for how we measure and predict psychological health.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Scholarly Communication
Developing models and tools to promote scholar-governed academic publishing. Co-founded [Open Scholar](https://openscholar.org.uk) in 2012. Research on replacing commercial journals with community-owned, open-access infrastructure.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Exercise, Brain & Cognition
Investigating whether and how physical exercise improves cognitive performance. Studies focus on sustained attention, brain oscillatory activity, cardiac interoception, and the neural mechanisms underlying exercise-cognition links.
:::

:::

## Recent Publications

| Year | Reference |
|------|-----------|
| 2025 | Goicoechea et al. **Bistability and affect shift dynamics in the prediction of psychological well-being.** *Emotion*, 25(4), 982–996. |
| 2024 | Yoris et al. **Delving into the relationship between regular physical exercise and cardiac interoception.** *Neuropsychologia*, 198, 108867. |
| 2023 | Ciria et al. **An umbrella review of randomized control trials on the effects of physical exercise on cognition.** *Nature Human Behaviour*, 7, 928–941. |
| 2023 | Brembs et al. **Replacing academic journals.** *Royal Society Open Science*, 10(7), 230206. |
| 2023 | Bernal & Perakakis. **No-pay publishing: use institutional repositories.** *Nature*, 619(7971), 698. |

[See all publications →](publications/index.qmd)

## Latest News

::: {.news-list}

**February 5, 2025** — [Rethinking how we measure affect: new insights into psychological well-being](blog/rethinking-affect.qmd)

**October 28, 2023** — [How to sell your journal: The secret business of academic journal acquisitions](blog/journal-acquisitions.qmd)

**September 2, 2023** — [Djokovic vs Science](blog/djokovic-vs-science.qmd)

:::

[See all posts →](blog/index.qmd)
